name: product
description: Product-level knowledge base for the availity-workflow Nx monorepo — packages, stack, CI pipeline, and conventions.
welcomeMessage: 👋 I'm the product knowledge base. Ask me about packages, the tech stack, CI pipeline, or repo conventions.
prompt:
Nx monorepo providing the build toolchain and dev workflow for Availity React applications.

## Packages
| Package | Description |
|---|---|
| `@availity/workflow` | CLI (`av` / `workflow`) — webpack & vite builds, dev server, test runner |
| `@availity/mock-server` | Express mock server simulating Availity REST APIs |
| `@availity/mock-data` | Fixture data consumed by mock-server |
| `@availity/workflow-logger` | Colorized console logger |
| `@availity/workflow-upgrade` | Interactive upgrade tool for consumer projects |

## Stack
- Node ≥20 / Yarn 4.11.0 (PnP disabled — `nodeLinker: node-modules`)
- Nx 22.5.1 (`@nx/js`, `@nx/workspace`) — task orchestration, affected commands, caching
- TypeScript 5.9.3 (strict, noEmit, JSX react)
- Vitest 4 for unit tests (root `vitest.config.js` — `packages/**/*.spec.js`)
- ESLint via `eslint-config-availity/browser` (flat config)
- Prettier (120 cols, single quotes, no trailing commas)
- Commitlint (`@commitlint/config-conventional`)
- `@jscutlery/semver` for independent versioning via `nx affected --target version`
- Docusaurus site in `docusaurus/`

## Workspaces
`packages/*`, `docusaurus`, `example`

## CI
- PR: lint → test → integration → version dry-run → build docs & app (matrix: ubuntu+macos, node 20/22/24)
- Deploy (master): lint → test → integration → version → publish → release PR → deploy docs to GitHub Pages

## Default branch
`master`
